critical.mandate: The `ci_cd.promotion_chain` block in global.yaml is the single source of truth for the promotion chain. Every write MUST pass the E20-S1 schema validator before being committed to disk.
The `id` field of every entry is immutable (ADR-033). Edits that attempt to change `id` must be rejected.
Every CRUD operation must be followed by a full chain re-validation. Partial updates are not permitted.
An operation that would leave zero entries in the chain is ALWAYS blocked. The chain must contain at least 1 environment at all times.
Remove and Edit operations must run the safety scan first — checkpoints, stories, test-environment.yaml — and require explicit user confirmation when references are found.

step.title: Load Current Promotion Chain
step.action: Read {project-root}/_gaia/_config/global.yaml in full. Preserve all fields and comments — this workflow only modifies the ci_cd.promotion_chain block.
Check whether a top-level ci_cd block exists and whether it contains a promotion_chain array.

check.if: ci_cd block absent or promotion_chain field missing

step.check: Display: "No promotion chain configured. Run /gaia-ci-setup first to create the initial chain, then re-run /gaia-ci-edit." HALT — do not attempt to add a chain from scratch.
step.action: Invoke the E20-S1 validator at {project-path}/test/validators/promotion-chain-validator.js on the current chain to confirm it is in a valid starting state. If the current chain is already invalid, surface the violations to the user before proceeding.

Render the current chain as a formatted table: position | id | branch | environment | merge_strategy | test_tiers | ci_provider. Display this table to the user so they see exactly what they are editing.

step.title: Present Operation Menu
step.action: Present the CRUD operation menu: [a] add — insert a new environment into the chain [r] remove — delete an existing environment (with safety scan) [e] edit — modify fields of an existing environment (id is immutable) [o] order — reorder the chain (warns on position 0 change) [v] view — re-render the current chain table and return to the menu [x] exit — close the workflow without writing
Re-render the chain table and loop back to this menu.
Write nothing — terminate the workflow cleanly.

step.title: Add Operation

critical.mandate: This step runs only when the user selected [a]dd.

step.action: Prompt the user for each required field in order: - id (string, slug format [a-z0-9-]+, must be unique in the chain) — required - name (human-readable label) — required - branch (git branch name, must be unique in the chain) — required - ci_provider — enum: github_actions | gitlab_ci | jenkins | circleci | azure_devops | bitbucket | none - merge_strategy — enum: merge | squash | rebase - test_tiers — optional array of tier names - auto_merge — boolean, default false - approval_required — boolean, default false
step.ask: Insertion position (0-based index, or leave blank to append to the end):
step.action: Call `addEnvironment(currentChain, newEntry, {position})` from {project-path}/test/validators/promotion-chain-editor.js. The helper rejects duplicate id or branch and returns a new chain array.
step.check: Display the specific field and value, prompt the user to enter a unique replacement, and re-run the helper. Repeat until the entry is accepted.
step.action: Proceed to Step 7 (Validate + Write + Cascade).

step.title: Remove Operation

critical.mandate: This step runs only when the user selected [r]emove.

step.action: Prompt the user for the id of the environment to remove.
step.check: HALT the operation: "Cannot remove the last environment — promotion chain must have at least 1 environment. Add a replacement environment first, or use /gaia-ci-setup to reset the chain." Return to Step 2.
step.action: Run the safety scan via scanReferences() using three source sets: 1. Checkpoints: enumerate {project-root}/_memory/checkpoints/ and {project-root}/_memory/checkpoints/completed/ for any dev-story-*.yaml whose `branch` field matches the target environment's branch. 2. Stories: glob {project-root}/docs/implementation-artifacts/*.md and grep for the environment id or branch name — collect matching story files. 3. test-environment.yaml: read {project-root}/_gaia/_config/test-environment.yaml and find any tier entry whose `environment` field equals the id being removed.
step.check: Display the list of references grouped by type (checkpoints, stories, test-environment tiers) and the specific detail for each. Present an explicit confirmation prompt: "The environment '{id}' is referenced by {N} location(s) above. Removing it may break downstream workflows. Proceed anyway? [y] Yes, remove and cascade / [n] No, cancel" Only on explicit [y] may the removal proceed.
step.action: Call `removeEnvironment(currentChain, targetId)` from the editor module. This helper re-checks the minimum-1 rule and throws MinimumChainError if it would leave zero entries.
Proceed to Step 7 (Validate + Write + Cascade).

step.title: Edit Operation

critical.mandate: This step runs only when the user selected [e]dit. The `id` field is immutable per ADR-033 — any attempt to modify it is rejected.

step.action: Prompt the user for the id of the environment to edit. Display its current field values.
Allow the user to modify any of the following fields: branch, name, environment, test_tiers, merge_strategy, auto_merge, approval_required, ci_provider, ci_checks.
step.check: Display: "Field 'id' is immutable (ADR-033). To rename the stable key, remove this entry and add a new one with the desired id." Block the edit and return the user to the field selection prompt.
step.action: Run the safety scan (same three sources as Step 4) BEFORE applying the edit, because an edited branch name has downstream consequences for CI pipelines and checkpoints.

check.if: scanReferences returns found==true AND branch is being edited

step.check: Warn the user that changing the branch may break active work and require explicit confirmation.
step.action: Call `editEnvironment(currentChain, targetId, updates)` from the editor module. The helper rejects immutable field changes and duplicate branch values.
Proceed to Step 7 (Validate + Write + Cascade).

step.title: Reorder Operation

critical.mandate: This step runs only when the user selected [o]rder. Changing position 0 changes the PR target for all feature branches — the user must explicitly acknowledge this.

step.action: Render the current chain as a numbered list and prompt the user to enter the new order as a comma-separated list of ids, e.g., "prod, dev, staging".

check.if: reorder would result in empty chain

step.check: HALT with MinimumChainError — chain must have at least 1 environment.
step.action: Call `reorderChain(currentChain, orderList)` from the editor module. The helper rejects unknown ids, rejects incomplete orderings, and returns a new chain array with a `.meta` property describing the position-0 change.
step.check: Warn the user: "Reordering position 0 changes the PR target from '{previous_pr_target}' to '{new_pr_target}'. All feature branches will merge into '{new_pr_target}' from this point forward. Continue? [y] Yes / [n] No" Only proceed on explicit [y].
step.action: Proceed to Step 7 (Validate + Write + Cascade).

step.title: Validate, Write, and Cascade Updates
step.action: Invoke the E20-S1 schema validator at {project-path}/test/validators/promotion-chain-validator.js on the modified chain. This enforces: minimum 1 entry, unique ids, unique branches, required fields, valid enum values, id slug pattern.
step.check: Display the list of violations returned by the validator and return the user to the operation menu without writing to global.yaml. The chain on disk remains unchanged.
step.action: Write the updated chain back to {project-root}/_gaia/_config/global.yaml. Only the ci_cd.promotion_chain block may be modified — all other fields and comments must be preserved exactly.

Cascade updates after the write succeeds: 1. If any branch name changed, update the matching trigger in {project-root}/.github/workflows/*.yml (or the equivalent CI config for the configured ci_provider). If no matching trigger exists, log a warning but do not fail. 2. If any environment id was removed or renamed, update {project-root}/_gaia/_config/test-environment.yaml tier mappings to drop or rename the affected entries. 3. Regenerate {project-root}/docs/test-artifacts/ci-setup.md by re-running the relevant section of /gaia-ci-setup (or noting the change with a manual update marker). 4. Run /gaia-build-configs to regenerate the .resolved/ config cache so the new chain is visible to all workflows.

On any cascade failure, surface the specific error to the user — do NOT attempt a rollback of global.yaml silently. Instead, report which cascade step failed and point the user at the file to correct manually.

step.title: Summary
step.action: Display a summary of what changed: operation performed, entries affected, whether position 0 moved, which cascade steps ran, and any warnings encountered during cascade.
Remind the user to commit the updated global.yaml and any cascade targets on a feature branch — this workflow never commits automatically.

next-step.standalone: This is a standalone workflow. Return to your current lifecycle phase.
